visa_free.nationality: EAC Member States (Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Uganda)
visa_free.notes: Citizens of EAC member states are not required to apply for a visa to enter Tanzania. EAC members listed on official immigration site.
SADC member countries (excluding Comoros) are visa-free. As listed on the official guidelines page: 'SADC member countries except Comoro'.

The official Tanzania immigration website had not updated its public-facing pages as of the validation date. Travelers should apply for e-visa in advance.

e_visa.notes: All countries listed in the VoA/e-visa list are eligible for online e-visa application through visa.immigration.go.tz. E-visa is the currently operative mechanism — multiple foreign government advisories (France, Canada) reported Tanzania suspended VoA effective January 30, 2025, making e-visa the de facto required route even though the official guidelines page had not been updated to reflect this as of the validation date. Processing time up to 10 business days. Fee schedule: Ordinary Visa USD 50 (single entry, 90 days), Multiple Entry USD 100 (12 months, max 90 days per stay), Business Visa USD 250, Transit Visa USD 30, Student Visa USD 50-550. Apply at: https://visa.immigration.go.tz

visa_policy.notes: Tanzania has three entry tiers: (1) Visa-free for EAC members, SADC members (except Comoros), and most Commonwealth nations (except UK, Canada, Nigeria, India, Pakistan, Australia, New Zealand); (2) E-visa required (previously also visa on arrival — VoA reportedly suspended as of January 30, 2025 per French and Canadian government advisories, though Tanzania's official guidelines page had not been updated as of June 2026 validation; travelers should apply for e-visa in advance at visa.immigration.go.tz); (3) Referral visa required for: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Burkina Faso, Chad, Eritrea, Guinea Conakry, Guinea Bissau, Iran, Iraq, Kyrgyzstan, Lebanon, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Palestine, Somalia, Syria, Sierra Leone, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, Yemen — must apply at least 2 months in advance, no VoA. Stateless persons and refugees also require referral visa. USA nationals must apply for Multiple Entry Visa (USD 100) instead of Ordinary Visa. Ghana appears in both visa-free and e-visa/VoA lists — visa-free classification applied.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found. Tanzania only offers citizenship by naturalization, which requires at minimum 12 months continuous residency preceded by 7 years (in aggregate) out of the preceding 10 years, knowledge of Kiswahili or English, good character, and economic/social/scientific contribution. Submission fee is USD 1,500 (non-refundable). Source: https://www.immigration.go.tz/index.php/3-0-citizenship-by-naturalization

programs.name: Residence Permit Class A - Investor
programs.notes: Class A permit for investors (self-employed, directors/shareholders of registered companies). Categories: A-1 Professional Artist Investor (USD 1,000 fee), A-2 Diaspora Investors (USD 1,000 fee), A-3 Other Sector/Industry Investors (USD 3,000 fee), A-4 Refugee Investors (USD 1,500 fee). Minimum investment capital not stated on the page — references minimum stipulated in the Investment Act. Requires TIC or ZIPA registration for investors.

programs.name: Residence Permit Class C-2 - Retired Persons
programs.notes: For persons retired in Tanzania or outside Tanzania with assured income to sustain themselves and dependents. Age must be 55 or above. Permit fee: USD 500 (USD 250 for EAC citizens and diaspora). Re-entry pass USD 50.

programs.name: Residence Permit Class C-11 - Buyer of Real Estate
programs.notes: For persons who buy houses in real estate but are not entitled to other available permit categories. Requires proof that value of purchased property is not less than USD 100,000. Requires letter of recommendation/qualification from Zanzibar Investment Promotion Authority (ZIPA) for Zanzibar or Tanzania Investment Centre (TIC) for Mainland. Certificate of occupancy/Title deed and tax clearance required. Permit fee: USD 500.

programs.name: Residence Permit Class B - Employment
programs.notes: Class B for foreigners with specific skills not readily available in local labor market. B-1 Specific employment (USD 2,000 fee), B-2 Diaspora employment (USD 500 fee), B-3 Short-term employment up to 6 months (USD 500), B-4 Refugee employment (USD 500). Requires Work Permit from Labour Commissioner.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, official/service passports of India may enter Tanzania without a visa for official travel. Agreement signed 10 July 2016 in Dar es Salaam, entered into force 26 December 2016.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of the People's Republic of China may enter Tanzania without a visa for up to 30 days. Bilateral mutual visa exemption agreement, effective 11 July 2005.

Holders of diplomatic, service and official passports of South Korea are exempt from Tanzania visa requirement for official travel. Bilateral agreement signed at State House Dar es Salaam. Gratis visa issued except when travelling in unofficial capacity.

validation_notes: ADDED 8 visa-free entries previously missing from the record: Ashmore & Cartier Islands, Cocos (Keeling) Islands, Heard Island and McDonald Islands, Niue, Norfolk Island, Ross Dependency, Saint Helena, Tokelau — all confirmed on www.immigration.go.tz/index.php/countries-which-are-not-required-to-apply-for-visa as of June 2026 validation

UPDATED visa_on_arrival notes for all ~110 entries: added advisory about VoA reportedly suspended as of January 30, 2025 per French and Canadian government advisories, even though the official Tanzania guidelines page (visa.immigration.go.tz/guidelines) still shows VoA as an option as of June 2026 validation — divergence flagged

UPDATED e_visa section: clarified that e-visa is now the de facto required route given VoA suspension, and added complete fee schedule (Ordinary USD 50, Multiple Entry USD 100, Business USD 250, Transit USD 30)
CORRECTED 'Swaziland (Eswatini)' nationality label to 'Eswatini (Swaziland)' to reflect official country name change in 2018
CORRECTED 'Bosnia' to 'Bosnia and Herzegovina' (full official name)
CORRECTED 'Central Africa Republic' to 'Central African Republic' (correct spelling)
CORRECTED 'East Timor (Timor-Leste)' label order to 'Timor-Leste (East Timor)' per official name

REMOVED duplicate Ghana entry from visa_on_arrival list — Ghana is already in visa_free (Commonwealth member) and the visa-free classification is the more privileged status; the duplicate VoA entry is redundant

VERIFIED referral visa list: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Burkina Faso, Chad, Eritrea, Guinea Bissau, Guinea Conakry, Iran, Iraq, Kyrgyzstan, Lebanon, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Palestine, Somalia, Sierra Leone, Syria, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, Yemen — confirmed on official immigration.go.tz page
VERIFIED cbi has_program=false, rbi programs, conditional_access entries — all confirmed unchanged
gaps: VoA suspension conflict: Multiple foreign government advisories (France, Canada) state Tanzania suspended VoA as of January 30, 2025, but the official Tanzania guidelines page (visa.immigration.go.tz/guidelines) still shows VoA as an option as of June 2026 validation — Tanzania has not issued an official update on its own immigration websites; travelers must verify before travel
Exact minimum investment capital for Residence Permit Class A not stated on the official immigration page — references 'minimum capital stipulated in the Investment Act' without specifying amount
Max stay days for visa-free nationals not explicitly stated on the official pages (typical default is 90 days per EAC/SADC protocols but not confirmed verbatim from the official page)
SADC Protocol on free movement is aspirational/partially implemented; actual visa-free treatment confirmed by Tanzania's own immigration page listing SADC members
No dedicated skilled worker / digital nomad / startup fast-track scheme found on official immigration pages
Dual citizenship policy for naturalization not explicitly stated on official page
Nigeria listed as excluded from Commonwealth visa-free but also appears in the referral visa category — exact treatment confirmed as referral required (not mere VoA/e-visa)
Max stay days for South Korea diplomatic/service/official passport holders not stated on the official Tanzania Embassy Seoul page — recorded as null
Brazil and Turkey bilateral diplomatic/service passport visa exemption agreements cited by aggregators but no direct official government-domain page confirmed — not recorded
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder entry rule found on any official Tanzania immigration source
No conditional access for holders of third-country visas (US/Schengen/UK/Canada) found on official Tanzania sources — absent by design

outbound_official.note: no official Tanzania government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Tanzania Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mfa.go.tz) is unreachable/down; the Immigration Department (immigration.go.tz) publishes only inbound visa policy; Tanzania Embassy portals (tzembassy.go.tz) focus on consular services and inbound visa types, not an outbound visa-free travel list for Tanzanian passport holders. No analog to India's MEA 'Visa Facility for Indian Nationals' page was found on any official .go.tz or .gov.tz domain.
